Use /compact to free up context when conversations get long
Press Shift+Tab to switch between chat and terminal input modes
Use @ to mention files directly in your prompt for better context
Try /cost to see token usage and cost for your current session
You can drag and drop images into the prompt for visual context
Use /clear to start fresh without closing the session
Press Esc to cancel a running Claude response
Try /help to see all available slash commands
You can pipe output into Claude: git diff | claude 'review this'
Use --print flag to get a single response without interactive mode
Claude can read PDFs, images, and Jupyter notebooks directly
Use /hooks to see all configured hooks and their status
Try /model to switch between different Claude models mid-session
Press Ctrl+C twice quickly to force-stop a response
You can use glob patterns with @ like @src/**/*.ts to mention multiple files
Claude Code respects .gitignore when searching your codebase
Use /bug to report issues directly from your session
Try running claude commit for AI-generated commit messages
You can configure custom slash commands in your settings
Use /status to see your current session info and permissions
Claude can run in headless mode for CI/CD pipelines
Try /fast to toggle fast mode for quicker responses
You can set up hooks to run scripts before or after tool executions
Use --resume to continue your last conversation
Try claude --help to see all CLI flags and options
You can configure per-project settings in .claude/settings.json
Use CLAUDE.md files to give Claude persistent context about your project
Try /plan to enter plan mode for complex multi-step tasks
You can use ! prefix to run shell commands inline during chat
Claude Code supports MCP servers for extending its capabilities
